page: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 7559 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in several categories. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score and database operations both achieving top-tier status. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 219.58 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 135.09 GB/sec. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 5006.15 tokens/sec on small 135M models, though performance drops to an average tier of 17.05 tokens/sec when processing larger 70B models.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity, this server offers a resource-dense profile for memory-bound and storage-heavy applications. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide predictable compute performance without hyperthreading overhead. This configuration is highly suitable for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive caching layers, data analytics, and medium-scale language model inference tasks that require significant local storage and memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure F64-32amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, configured with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 16.0 GB per core, alongside 7559 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and outstanding database operation speeds. Benchmark results show strong memory bandwidth, with large-block uncached reads at 219.58 GB/sec and writes at 135.09 GB/sec. While small-model LLM inference is highly efficient, processing larger 70B models drops to average tiers. This resource-dense, high-memory configuration is optimized for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics workloads.
